source: InsideAirbnb Austin 2023; ~15,000 active listings, ~240k housing units
notes: SXSW, ACL, tourism, and UT game day demand sustain year-round STR market; significant unit removal from long-term pool near campus

source: InsideAirbnb LA 2023; ~40,000 listings in city limits
notes: Massive tourism market; Westwood/UCLA area has elevated STR despite city regulations; units removed from student rental pool
Tech hub tourism + outdoor recreation; near UW campus in Capitol Hill/University District has moderate STR presence

source: InsideAirbnb Boulder 2023; outdoor recreation and conference tourism
notes: CU Boulder market; ski proximity and Flatirons tourism sustain year-round STR; compounds with U+3 ordinance to tighten long-term supply

source: InsideAirbnb Nashville 2023; one of highest STR densities in US
notes: Bachelorette/tourism capital; Vanderbilt area competes heavily with STR for available units; major long-term supply compression
UM market; massive tourism STR concentration; Coral Gables itself is lower but broader Miami supply pool is heavily STR-compressed

source: InsideAirbnb Gainesville 2023 estimate; limited tourism base
notes: UF market; STR activity is mostly game-day weekend rentals; not a persistent supply compression factor year-round

source: InsideAirbnb Athens GA 2023 estimate; growing music/arts tourism
notes: UGA market; growing Airbnb presence driven by music scene and football; more persistent than typical college town but not dominant

source: InsideAirbnb Charlottesville 2023 estimate; wine tourism + UVA events
notes: UVA market; wine country tourism + graduation/events sustain moderate STR activity; some year-round supply compression
UMich market; game-day STR spikes are significant but off-season is light; moderate average annual impact on long-term supply
Texas A&M market; STR mostly game-day use; not a tourist destination between football season; minimal long-term supply impact
VTech market; small town with limited tourism; STR activity almost exclusively game-day football; no persistent year-round compression
Game-day Penn State STR spikes are large in aggregate but concentrated in 8 home game weekends; year-round impact is low
UIUC market; no tourism draw outside of university events; minimal STR activity year-round
KU market; small market with limited tourism; STR is niche
ASU market; Phoenix MSA has large STR market driven by sports tourism (Super Bowl, spring training, March Madness); Tempe itself moderately affected
Ohio State market; large city with moderate STR driven by sports events and corporate travel; campus area impact is limited relative to total supply
LSU market; LSU game days + New Orleans proximity drive moderate STR; some year-round impact

source: InsideAirbnb Knoxville 2023 estimate; Smoky Mountains gateway
notes: UT Knoxville market; Gatlinburg/Smokies tourism spills over; Knoxville itself has growing STR market as gateway city
Alabama market; game-day spikes notable but year-round STR activity is low
Auburn University market; limited tourism; STR is almost exclusively football game-day use
UMN market; large metro with business travel + sports/arts tourism; UMN area has modest STR; not a dominant factor
UW-Madison market; state capital + Big Ten sports draw moderate STR; persistent but not dominant
U of Arkansas market; Walmart HQ brings some business travel STR but overall market is light
Oklahoma State market; minimal tourism; STR is game-day only
University of Iowa market; limited outside tourism; game-day STR only
Mizzou market; no major tourism draw; minimal STR activity
FSU/FAMU market; state capital has some business travel STR; limited tourism
Kansas State market; extremely limited tourism; STR is negligible year-round
UC Berkeley market; Bay Area tourism + tech hub events sustain high STR; significant supply compression in a market already constrained by zoning
